title: OMP Audit Trace
description: The complete structured evidence record produced by every OMP routing event. Defined in draft-veridom-omp-00 Section 7 (IETF Datatracker: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-veridom-omp/). Every field is REQUIRED unless marked optional. The sealed Audit Trace is the primary regulatory evidence artifact of an OMP deployment.

trace_id.description: UUID v4. Globally unique identifier for this Audit Trace record.

trace_version.description: Schema version of this trace record.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC with millisecond precision. Wall-clock time at which Stage S5 (trace sealing) completed.

vertical.description: The regulated vertical or domain for this deployment. Examples: 'kenya_dcp', 'kenya_sacco', 'uk_fca_consumer_duty', 'eu_ai_act', 'us_clinical_ai', 'us_employment_ads', 'us_mortgage_fhfa'.

deployment_id.description: Stable identifier for the specific OMP deployment instance. Set at deployment initialisation and constant across all traces in the deployment.

schema_version.description: OMP Audit Trace schema version identifier. Implementations extending the schema MUST use a distinct schema_version string.

interaction_id.description: UUID v4. Unique identifier for the interaction that generated this trace. Assigned at Stage S1 ingestion.

interaction_received.description: ISO 8601 UTC. Timestamp at which Stage S1 received the interaction payload.

interaction_hash.description: SHA-256 of the RFC 8785 canonical form of the interaction payload. Computed at Stage S1. Proves input data integrity — any subsequent modification to the payload is detectable.

channel.description: The channel through which the interaction was received. Examples: 'api', 'mobile_app', 'agent_portal', 'batch_process'.

requester_id.description: Anonymised or pseudonymised identifier for the entity that submitted the interaction. MUST NOT contain unredacted personal data.

intent_class.description: The Intent Class to which Stage S2 classified this interaction. Must be a registered Intent Class for the vertical.

intent_confidence.description: Classifier confidence in the intent_class assignment. Float [0.0, 1.0]. A value below 0.60 triggers FORCE_ASSISTED per the routing invariant.

intent_top3.description: Top-3 candidate Intent Classes with their confidence scores.

classifier_version.description: Version identifier of the intent classifier used at Stage S2. Required for model risk governance and decision reconstructability.

confidence_score.description: Composite Confidence Score. Weighted combination of C_m (model confidence), C_p (policy compliance), and C_d (data completeness). This is the value compared to routing_threshold in the routing decision.

confidence_components.description: Decomposed confidence score components enabling auditability of the routing decision.

C_m.description: Model confidence. The AI system's own confidence in its output for this interaction.

C_p.description: Policy compliance score. Evaluated by the Watchtower framework. A value of 0.0 MUST force ESCALATED routing.

C_d.description: Data completeness score. Fraction of required input fields present and valid.

weights.description: Weights applied to each component. Must sum to 1.0.

routing_threshold.description: The configured theta value for this vertical/deployment — the minimum confidence_score required for AUTONOMOUS routing. Recorded per-trace to enable retrospective routing verification.

watchtowers_evaluated.description: Complete list of all Watchtowers evaluated for this interaction at Stage S3, including those that did not trigger. ALL Watchtowers evaluated MUST be recorded regardless of whether they triggered.

triggered.description: Whether this Watchtower triggered for this interaction.

evidence.description: Watchtower-specific evidence object documenting the evaluation result. Structure varies by Watchtower definition.

evaluated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of Watchtower evaluation.

watchtower_override.description: True if any Watchtower overrode the confidence-score-based routing decision. Highest-severity Watchtower result governs: HARD_BLOCK > FORCE_ASSISTED > AUDIT_ONLY.

override_watchtower_id.description: ID of the Watchtower whose result governed the routing decision. Required key. Null if no override occurred (routing was determined by confidence_score alone).

routing_path.description: The routing outcome. Exactly one of the three OMP routing states. This is the primary accountability field: AUTONOMOUS (AI decided without human review), ASSISTED (Named Accountable Officer reviewed before dispatch), ESCALATED (mandatory human intervention required).

routing_rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of the routing decision with explicit rule reference. Must identify whether routing was determined by confidence_score comparison to routing_threshold, a Watchtower trigger, or both.

routing_decided_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the routing decision at Stage S4.

autonomous_dispatch.description: Required key. Populated only for AUTONOMOUS routing. Contains dispatch confirmation and any post-dispatch metadata. Null for ASSISTED and ESCALATED.

assisted_resolution.description: Required key. Populated only for ASSISTED routing. REQUIRED for ASSISTED outcomes. Null for AUTONOMOUS and ESCALATED.

named_accountable_officer_id.description: Stable identifier for the Named Accountable Officer who reviewed this interaction.

review_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of NAO review action.

sla_met.description: Whether the NAO review was completed within the configured SLA window.

escalation_record.description: Required key. Populated only for ESCALATED routing. REQUIRED for ESCALATED outcomes. Null for AUTONOMOUS and ASSISTED.

escalation_reason.description: Documented reason for escalation — Watchtower ID, confidence floor breach, or other trigger.

escalation_authority_id.description: Identifier of the human authority to whom this interaction was escalated.

tst_time.description: ISO 8601 UTC with millisecond precision. The time attested by the Timestamp Authority. This is the legally significant timestamp: it proves the Audit Trace existed in its exact form at this moment, attested by a party external to the institution. Cannot be retroactively obtained for a fabricated record.

tst_tsa_identity.description: Distinguished Name (DN) of the Timestamp Authority that issued this TimeStampToken. For EU deployments under the EUAIA profile, MUST identify a QTSP listed in an EU Member State trusted list under eIDAS Article 22.

tst_raw.description: Base64-encoded raw RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (DER-encoded TSTInfo structure). Used by the OMP Reference Validator to verify TST authenticity against the TSA's public key.

tst_serial.description: Serial number of the TimeStampToken. Unique within the issuing TSA.

tst_hash_alg.description: Hash algorithm used in the TimeStampToken message imprint. MUST be SHA-256.

tst_message_imprint.description: SHA-256 hash value in the TimeStampToken message imprint. It is expected to equal trace_content_hash, and that cross-field equality is verified by the OMP Reference Validator rather than by JSON Schema alone.

tst_certificate.description: Base64-encoded TSA signing certificate or URI to the TSA certificate. Required for offline verification by the OMP Reference Validator.

source_state_hash.description: Required key. SHA-256(canonical_form(external_api_response) || query_timestamp_ms). Captures the exact state of an external data source at the millisecond of query. Null if no external source was queried. See Section 8.1 of draft-veridom-omp-00 for computation method.

trace_content_hash.description: H_content = SHA-256 of the canonical form of this Audit Trace record (all fields except trace_content_hash, trace_chain_hash, and seal fields). This is the primary tamper-evidence field: any post-sealing modification to any trace field is detectable by recomputing this hash.

trace_chain_hash.description: H_c = SHA-256(H_content_this || H_c_previous). Merkle chain entry linking this trace to all preceding traces in the deployment. Any modification to any historical trace invalidates all subsequent chain hashes. Enables completeness verification: a gap in the chain signals a deleted or missing trace.

sealed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which Stage S5 completed the sealing process.

seal_version.description: Identifier of the sealing mechanism version used. Constant for this specification version.

$comment: additionalProperties is true to allow domain profile extensions (EUAIA, DutyMark, InsureMark, etc.). Profile-specific fields are defined in the respective domain profile schemas. All profile extension fields SHOULD use a profile-specific prefix (e.g., 'euaia_', 'fca_', 'ins_') to avoid collisions.
